title: aconitum × bujbense
intro: Aconitum × bujbense, a hybrid of the Aconitum genus, is a rare and highly toxic flowering plant native to the Korean peninsula and Japan. It belongs to the Ranunculaceae family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Eastern medicine. The plant is rich in alkaloids, including aconitine, which has anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antiviral properties, providing health benefits such as pain relief, fever reduction, and improved immune function. Aconitum × bujbense is typically prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, but caution is advised due to its toxicity, and it should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
